format_note: Canonical open-operator-asks file (away-ops-rules.md token mechanics). Every entry carries the EXACT reply string(s); the operator replies in the Slack bot channel and the 62.2 handler records it to handoff/operator_tokens.jsonl.

asks.due: 62.7 dress rehearsal (already on the rehearsal checklist) OR any earlier real token
asks.ask: 62.2 (operator-token handler) is verified for criteria 1+2 (handler-above-catch-all + quoted grammar + append; operator-user+channel allowlist; 29 unit tests; pytest exit 0). Criterion 3 is the LIVE round-trip and is the only thing left to flip 62.2 to done: it needs ONE real operator Slack message because handoff/operator_tokens.jsonl is created only by the running bot (a synthetic line was deliberately NOT written -- it would fabricate operator evidence and a stale RESUME could re-fire). Send the drill token from your phone in the approvals channel (C0ANTGNNK8D). It is NOT in KNOWN_TOKEN_ENV_MAP, so it is recorded only -- NO .env change, NO trading effect. Expected threaded ACK: 'OPERATOR TOKEN RECORDED (operator_tokens.jsonl line 1): `TEST TOKEN: PING` -- the next away session acts on it.' The next session then pastes the jsonl line + ACK permalink into live_check_62.2.md and a fresh Q/A flips 62.2.
asks.recommended: TEST TOKEN: PING (sent in C0ANTGNNK8D during the 62.7 rehearsal; closes 62.2's last criterion and proves the whole token pipeline end-to-end)

asks.due: optional (NON-BLOCKING; away sessions already auto-override to Opus 4.8 per-spawn -- they keep working)
asks.ask: The Layer-3 harness subagents researcher (.claude/agents/researcher.md) + qa (.claude/agents/qa.md) are pinned model: claude-fable-5 (phase-59.1). In headless 'claude -p' away sessions, that pin FAILS to spawn ('model may not exist or you may not have access'). Cycle 64 worked around it by overriding each spawn to model: opus (the away-session-pinned model + the subagents' pre-59.1 config), so the research gate + Q/A both ran normally. This will recur on EVERY away AM/PM session for the 3-week window. Options: (A) leave the per-spawn Opus override in place (zero config change; works fine; Opus is the documented pre-59.1 + away-session model) -- RECOMMENDED; (B) repin both agent files to model: opus for the away window (requires a session restart to take effect, and is an agent-roster behavior change the plan explicitly deferred -- 'Researcher/Q/A subagent pins stay as committed'), so a session should only do this on your token.
reply_options: FABLE HEADLESS: REPIN AGENTS TO OPUS
asks.recommended: FABLE HEADLESS: KEEP OPUS OVERRIDE (no config churn; the override is grounded in the away-ops Opus-4.8 session pin and the subagents' pre-59.1 config; revisit Fable on return)

asks.due: 2026-06-15 (HARD: the Agent SDK credit regime starts that day, 3 days into the away window)
asks.ask: From 2026-06-15, headless claude -p sessions draw a separate monthly Agent SDK credit (Max 5x = $100/mo, Max 20x = $200/mo). Two Opus sessions/day for 3 weeks may exceed it. On exhaustion, scheduled sessions STOP until the credit refreshes -- unless usage credits are enabled, which is metered spend (conflicts with the $0 decision). The wrapper already logs per-session cost + LIMIT_HIT lines so the digest shows burn from day 1.
reply_options: SDK CREDIT: ENABLE USAGE CREDITS <monthly cap USD>
asks.recommended: SDK CREDIT: STOP-ON-EXHAUSTION (consistent with the $0 decision; worst case dev pauses until the monthly refresh while trading + digests continue)

asks.ask: ~/Library/LaunchAgents/com.pyfinagent.mas-harness.plist is bootout'd but NOT disabled and still on disk -- a reboot + auto-login re-bootstraps its 30-minute loop, racing the away sessions on a different lockfile. Away sessions are hook-blocked from launchctl disable on pyfinagent labels (the 62.0 guard, working as designed), so only you can neutralize it: mv ~/Library/LaunchAgents/com.pyfinagent.mas-harness.plist ~/Library/LaunchAgents/disabled.com.pyfinagent.mas-harness.plist.bak
asks.recommended: MAS PLIST: MOVED (run the mv during the 62.7 dress rehearsal)

asks.due: optional (return-day OK; away paging works via the bot-token fallback)
asks.ask: settings.slack_webhook_url is EMPTY, which silently disables webhook-path P1 paging incl. the legacy backend_watchdog.sh:64 alert. Away-window paging is COVERED by the new bot-token fallback (live-proven). To resurrect the webhook path too: create a Slack incoming webhook and add SLACK_WEBHOOK_URL=<url> to backend/.env (keystroke), then restart backend + bot.
asks.recommended: WEBHOOK: SKIP (bot-token fallback is live-proven; do on return)

asks.due: operator's discretion (nightly stays $0 preflight-only until then)
asks.ask: langchain-huggingface stack installed (constrained; langchain-core held at 1.2.30) -- the nightly autoresearch cron is FIXED and exits 0, but full GPTResearcher runs would spend ~$0.10-0.50/night on Anthropic API. The nightly is pinned to --preflight-only ($0) for the away window. To resume full nightly memos: reply the token; the session removes the flag from scripts/autoresearch/run_nightly.sh.
asks.recommended: AUTORESEARCH SPEND: KEEP PREFLIGHT-ONLY (consistent with $0; resume on return)

asks.ask: The 2026-06-12 morning paste left an unbalanced quote in a backend/.env comment (line ~81) -- harmless to pydantic but it broke shell-sourcing (killed the autoresearch nightly silently; run_nightly.sh now hardened). Cleanup keystroke: open backend/.env, fix/delete the wrapped comment line ending in a stray quote (the three PAPER_*=true lines below it are correct -- do not touch them).
